methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $38M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Helical Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Takaya Taguchi and Junichi Miyazawa, having raised $38M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 private peers (including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power) and 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR), all operating within the nuclear energy sector.

Stage-progression analysis serves as the primary valuation method, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $38M total raised proxy, yielding $76M. Peer-median comparison was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer coverage—while 8 same-sector peers exist, only one same-stage and one adjacent-stage comparable were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable cross-sectional inference. A broader sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology maturity and capital intensity across the peer set.

The triangulated valuation range is $65M (low) to $87M (high), centered on a $76M median. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for frontier nuclear capital requirements and the absence of transaction precedent data specific to this technology subset. Additional fundraising activity or technical milestones would be necessary to calibrate forward-stage expectations more precisely.
